query: mixed color temperature sodium vapor orange streetlight clashing fluorescent night urban
message: anti_aesthetics/lighting: mixed color temp — sodium vapor orange vs blue twilight, clashing artificial light sources, desolate urban night

response.objective_caption: A nighttime view of a multi-lane highway with bright extended streaks of white and red light from vehicle headlights and taillights. A concrete sidewalk runs along the right side of the frame, separated from the asphalt by a low curb. On the opposite side of the road, a grassy median with leafless trees extends toward an elevated pedestrian bridge that crosses the highway in the mid-ground. Street lamps line the roadway, casting a pervasive yellow-orange glow across the pavement and surrounding grass. In the distant background, clusters of blue and white lights shine near low buildings beneath a dark, overcast sky.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: A nighttime view of a multi-lane highway where a sickly yellow-orange sodium vapor cast from street lamps blankets the roadway, sidewalk, and vegetation in an unhealthy hue. Vehicle headlights and taillights appear as severely overexposed streaks of white and red light, blown out into featureless bright ribbons that dominate the road surface. An elevated pedestrian bridge spans the highway in the mid-ground, flanked by leafless trees in a grassy median. The scene suffers from color inconsistency, as cool blue and white lights visible in the distant background clash against the warm yellow tones, creating uneven white balance and breaking tonal coherence across the frame.
